## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

This is a pure output/display change — no gallops data-schema or serialization change.        
5d7dfe3e2  ₣BD       Add semantic-linefeed guidance for paddock/docket prose. claude-jjk-core.md gains a 'Semantic linefeeds in artifact prose' posture rule (one sentence per line, long sentences broken at major internal boundaries; markdown soft-wraps so it is render-invisible and free, the payoff being clean incremental diffs of paddock/docket edits; word-grain diffs come from git diff --word-diff, not physical word-splitting). JJS0 gains a sibling '=== Diff-Friendly Prose' design principle right after 'Diff-Friendly Mutations', closing that principle's prose-layer gap — the existing one covered only JSON structure (coronet embedding) while paddock/docket prose is git-diffed on every edit. The two are layered: core.md is the actionable how-to, JJS0 the design rationale it cites. JJS0's own source already follows semantic linefeeds, so the convention matches house style; the new spec block is itself authored in semantic linefeeds. Both linked terms (jjsz_gazette, jjdgr_gallops) resolve against the mapping section.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
3dca94651  ₢BDAAP    jjx_drop now echoes its prior→new state transition (₢…: rough → abandoned) additively above the preserved commit-hash line, so a successful drop confirms its own effect without needing jjx_show — the non-schema half of the drop-legibility pace. Consolidated the PaceState→display-label mapping to a single canonical home: jjrg_PaceState::jjrg_as_str() referencing new JJRG_STATE_* consts in jjrt_types.rs (per RCG String Boundary Discipline, distinct from the jjgte_* serde wire form), eliminating the duplicated match in scout and ops and a tautology test. Removed the redundant zjrsc_pace_state_str helper and its four orphaned tests; upgraded the jjtq tautology into a real all-variant jjrg_as_str test against the consts. Build clean under deny(warnings); 333 JJK tests pass. The brief/coronets abandoned-markers (Secondary) remain deferred as a deliberate output-format change.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
71a6eceea  ₢BDAAQ    Consolidate the heat-identity reference rules with a third member: bar silks from both paddock and docket prose. Adds the "Silks never appear in artifact prose" rule alongside the existing coronet-in-paddock discipline, framing firemark/coronet/silks as a three-way distinction by lifecycle stability — firemarks survive everywhere, coronets are barred from paddocks (cross-ref dockets excepted), silks are barred from both.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BD
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Tactical heat for V4-era JJK improvements that are ready to mount — settled designs, mechanical fixes, and concrete spook repairs. Carved out of ₣Am (jjk-v5-notional) on 260507 to surface unblocked work that was hidden in the parking lot.
6	
7	## Character

6	
7	# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
12	home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
13	sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
14	(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
15	*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
16	already exists: point at it, never restate it.
17	
18	ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
19	in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
20	than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
21	and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.
22	
23	ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
24	does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
25	candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
26	in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
27	not repair.
28	
29	## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog
30	
31	ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:
32	
33	- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
34	  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
35	  number.
36	- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
37	  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
38	  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
39	  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
42	discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.
43	
44	## The spine — reference the home
45	
46	The governing idea, stated once:
47	
48	> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
49	> quoin-refs.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs source's duty to reference
50	> rather than recreate.
51	
52	This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
53	recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
54	risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
55	between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
56	Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
57	ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
58	single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
59	goes.*
60	
61	## The three homes — allocate by when-read
62	
63	A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:
64	
65	| When read | Home | Carries |
66	|-----------|------|---------|
67	| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
68	| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
69	| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
70	
71	Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
72	execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
73	of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
74	announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
75	by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.
76	
77	Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:
78	
79	- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
80	  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
81	- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
82	  is doing. Read when running it.
83	
84	Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
85	practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
86	announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
87	announcement of the contract, not contamination.
88	
89	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
90	
91	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
92	
93	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
94	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
95	
96	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
97	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
98	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
99	
100	Three rules ride that litmus:
101	
102	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
103	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
104	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
105	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
106	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
107	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
108	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
109	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
110	   recreated.
111	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
112	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
113	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
114	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
115	
116	## The named home — the conformance fixture
117	
118	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
119	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
120	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
121	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
122	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
123	
124	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
125	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
126	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
127	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
128	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
129	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
130	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
131	
132	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
133	is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
134	by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
135	(move discipline, rule 1).
136	
137	## The Move Catalog
138	
139	Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
140	**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
141	**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
142	mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
143	detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
144	101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.
145	
146	### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant
147	
148	- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
149	  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
150	  should. Not human-facing prose.
151	- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
152	  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
153	  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
154	- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
155	  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
156	- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
157	  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
158	- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
159	  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
160	- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
161	  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
162	  Exclude that class before sweeping.
163	- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
164	  application of this move.
165	
166	### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity
167	
168	- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
169	  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
170	- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
171	  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
172	  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
173	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
174	  exists.
175	- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
176	  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
177	- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
178	- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
179	  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
180	  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
181	  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
182	  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
183	  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
184	  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
185	  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).
186	
187	### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead
188	
189	- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
190	  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
191	- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
192	  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
193	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
194	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
195	- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
196	  mutated.
197	
198	## The AXLA/MCM interface
199	
200	### The lookahead scanning rule
201	
202	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
203	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
204	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
205	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
206	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
207	
208	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
209	
210	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
211	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
212	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
213	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
214	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
215	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
216	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
217	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
218	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
219	
220	## Related Guides
221	
222	- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
223	  bash instances of the spine.
224	- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
225	  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
226	- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
227	- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
228	- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
229	  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
230	- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
231	  themselves.
232	
233	## Acronym Registry
234	
235	| Term | Expansion |
236	|------|-----------|
237	| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
238	| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs |
239	| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
240	| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
241	| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
242	| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
243	| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |
244

3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.

## The named home — the conformance fixture

Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.

The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.

**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
(move discipline, rule 1).

## The Move Catalog

Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.

### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant

- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
  should. Not human-facing prose.
- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
  Exclude that class before sweeping.
- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
  application of this move.

### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity

- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
  exists.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).

### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead

- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
  mutated.

## The AXLA/MCM interface

### The lookahead scanning rule

When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
distinct token type and does not shift that count.

### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)

ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.

## Related Guides

- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
  bash instances of the spine.
- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
  themselves.

## Acronym Registry

| Term | Expansion |
|------|-----------|
| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs |
| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |

# JJK Kit Source

Edits here apply to the kit source only. Do not modify the active installation in `.claude/` or the parent project's CLAUDE.md.

lastPrompt: I believe that yesterday we invented an ACG that might relate to this.  Read it and some of these documents and assess relevance.

content.text:
Read all five. Here's my honest assessment — and yes, ACG is relevant, in a specific and useful way, but not the way "spec hygiene" might first suggest.

## Verdict

ACG is relevant as a **content criterion** that sharpens this pace's done-condition — not as a restructuring mandate. Rail-fidelity (the docket's current template) is a *structural* test: are the sections present (Invocation / arguments / stdout / behavior / validation-errors)? ACG supplies the *content* test those sections should pass. The two compose cleanly.

## Where it bites (in scope)

**1. The spec is the design-time home — so fidelity means "carries design-time knowledge," not "carries everything."** ACG's three-homes table says the spec holds *what a thing is and why it's shaped so*; edit-time mechanics go in source comments, runtime strings go in announcements. The thin specs (especially `JJSCGS-get-spec.adoc`, 33 lines) are defective because design-time knowledge is *missing from its only correct home*. But ACG also guards the other direction: if "filling to rail-fidelity" tempts me to dump the Rust's exact control flow or echo literal output strings into the spec, that's *temporal misallocation* — fidelity turning into a junk drawer. So ACG both motivates the fill and bounds it.

**2. Reference the home, don't recreate — via quoin-refs.** This is the actionable discipline while writing. Saddle already models it well: `Get last 10 steeplechase entries … via {jjdo_log} logic`, `Output format matches {jjdo_show} heat view` — it points at the home instead of restating it. When I fill get-spec and the thin sections, the move is to *prefer quoin-refs* (`{jjdo_show}`, `{jjdt_coronet}`, `{jjdr_load}`) over freshly-written prose that re-describes a homed concept.

## Where it bites but ACG itself says *don't* (out of scope)

The file-touch-bitmap and commit-swim-lane blocks are **recreated nearly verbatim across saddle, parade, and show** — saddle references `{jjdo_show}` for the *description* but still copies the full *example*; parade copies both. That duplication is the textbook ACG spine violation: one format, three homes. **But** — ACG's own move-discipline rule 1 ("never sweep vocabulary a hot heat is still moving") and its detect-only licensing for document-side moves both say: *flag it, don't fix it here.* The jjx_show end-state is owned by the show-rework pace in this same heat (₣BD). Consolidating the bitmap home now would collide with that pace and is exactly the move ACG forbids mid-flight.

## Net

ACG doesn't change the pace's *scope* — it stays "fill the thin sections to rail-fidelity, no code change." It changes the *standard*: fill each section with design-time knowledge homed nowhere else, reaching for quoin-refs over recreated prose, and leave the cross-spec bitmap duplication as a noted detect-only finding for the show-rework pace to own.

Want me to fold this into the docket as a one-line `## Standard` note (cite ACG as the content criterion), or just carry it as how I execute the pace? I'd lean toward carrying it — the docket's already mount-ready and a citation risks over-prescription.

message.content: One interesting collision here is that ACG has an idea of 'stdout' but job jockey largely works as an MCP server without stdout, which makes me think that ACG needs to get more nuanced about 'info' and 'trace' and 'error' texts and such loggers.  Thoughts?

content.text:
You've found a real hole, and I think it's sharper than "ACG should add some logger rows." Let me give you the honest shape of it, including where I'd push back on the obvious fix.

## What's actually conflated

ACG organizes by *when-read*, but the deeper thing under each home is a **forcing function** — a reason someone re-reads it and would catch drift. The spec gets re-read when design is questioned; the announcement gets re-read *every run*, so a wrong announcement is caught by the human watching it. That re-read-every-run forcing function is the entire reason the "execution-time → announcement" home is trustworthy.

The "stdout" in these specs quietly rides two different things at once, and a CLI tool lets you get away with it because they're the same stream:

1. **The product** — the result the command *returns*. For these JJK specs, `{jjds_stdout}` is a *return contract*, not an announcement. Its shape is part of what the command *is* — it's already design-time-homed (it literally lives in the spec). The word "printed" in ACG's three-homes table is what miscategorizes it as execution-time.
2. **The announcement** — "what's happening now," genuinely execution-time, re-read every run.

For a CLI filter those collapse into one stream. **MCP breaks the collapse open**, which is exactly what you noticed. JJK has at least *three* execution-time media and ACG's single "announcement" home covers none of them:
- the **MCP tool result** (the inline return — the product),
- **`gazette_out.md`** (a filesystem side-channel for bulk product too big for the result),
- the **server's info/trace/error logs** (operator-facing, out of band, the agent never sees them).

## The part that's genuinely new

Your `info`/`trace`/`error` instinct is right, but the load-bearing distinction isn't the *severity ladder* — it's **does this output have a forcing function**:

- `error`/diagnostic — read by the caller/operator *at failure time*. Has a forcing function (failures get looked at). Announcement-like — this is the corner ACG already gestures at with the missing-parameter option-disclosure blessing.
- `info` at a human terminal — re-read every run → a true announcement, ACG's current model.
- `trace`/`debug` to a log file — **write-mostly, read only when something breaks, no re-read-every-run forcing function.** In rot terms it behaves *like an edit-time comment*: conceptual knowledge dumped into a trace log rots exactly as badly as the same knowledge dumped into a comment, and for the same reason — nobody re-reads it under a forcing function. That's the insight ACG is currently missing, and it's the one that actually matters.

## Where I'd push back

I would *not* let ACG grow a row per log level (info/warn/debug/trace/error). That's importing the logging library's taxonomy, which is RCG/BCG territory — per-language idiom — and ACG enumerating it would violate its own load-bearing test. ACG's axis is *who-reads / forcing-function*, not severity. The right amendment is to **split the execution-time row into product vs. trace, and state that trace without a forcing function rots like a comment** — a generalization of the existing axis, not a new ladder grafted on.

## The loop back to the mounted pace

This isn't idle — it touches ₢BDAAN directly. The docket lists "output channel" as one of the four things these thin specs must document, and every one of them currently says `{jjds_stdout}`. For an MCP command that term is a CLI-ism: the real channel is *MCP tool result*, sometimes *plus gazette side-channel*. So getting ACG's execution-time model right would actually inform how I write the "output channel" section in these very specs — whether `{jjds_stdout}` is even the honest quoin for an MCP return.

Two governance notes so I don't overstep: an ACG amendment is its own pace in CMK, and the CMK covenant says that document changes only in conversation, never by silent edit — so this *is* where it'd legitimately start, but I won't touch ACG now. And it's upstream of the mounted JJK pace; I'd resolve the `{jjds_stdout}` question for these four specs at execution time regardless of whether ACG itself changes.

Want to keep developing the ACG side, or pin this as a captured itch/pace and return to mounting ₢BDAAN?

message.content: I have a strong counter reaction.  I intend ACG to be the template for strongly and appropriately specifying critical sequences named as routines, language independent.  In my experience of engineering, the logging themes are recurring and critical, so naming them and having a templatized nature for them though with customized consequences and plumbing paths per project is appropriate.

content.text:
You're right, and I had the partition line in the wrong place. Let me say where I was wrong, precisely, because the *reason* matters more than the concession.

## What I conflated

I lumped two different things together and rejected both: **naming the output roles** (info, trace, error, product) and **importing the per-language severity ladder** (DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR as a verbosity knob). The second is genuinely per-language idiom. But the first — the *semantic output roles* of a routine — is exactly the kind of recurring, language-independent structure a routine-spec template should name. I rejected the roles by association with the ladder. That was wrong: the roles are the universal; the ladder and the sink are the binding. You drew the line at role-vs-binding, and that's the correct line — I'd drawn it at role-vs-no-role and shoved roles out to RCG.

And the boundary I was actually trying to protect — "don't hardcode language idiom into ACG" — *survives intact* under your framing, because it's the binding that stays per-project, which is precisely your "customized consequences and plumbing paths." We don't disagree on the boundary. I just had the names of the roles on the wrong side of it.

## How my earlier point folds in rather than competes

The forcing-function analysis stops being a *rival organizing axis* and becomes a **per-role invariant the template carries**. Each named role gets a forcing-function property, which is what makes the template prescriptive rather than decorative:

- **Product** (the return) — exercised every call; the caller is the forcing function. Strong.
- **Announcement / info** — re-read every run *if human-facing*; weak-to-none if only logged.
- **Trace / diagnostic** — write-mostly, no forcing function → **must not be the sole home of design-time knowledge** (rots like a comment). This is the invariant worth attaching.
- **Error** — read at failure; moderate. Carries ACG's already-blessed option-disclosure.

So "trace" earns its name *and* a rule that travels with it. That's the templatized nature doing work.

## A strawman to react to

Language-independent role on the left; the per-project plumbing is the bound column, varying freely:

| Role | Carries | bash | Rust | **MCP (JJK)** | cloud step |
|------|---------|------|------|---------------|------------|
| **Product** | the routine's yield | stdout | return value | **tool result / `gazette_out.md`** | build output |
| **Announce/info** | intent, now | stderr | log macro | **server log** | step log |
| **Trace** | debug breadcrumbs | — | log file | **server log file** | step log |
| **Error** | failure + options | stderr+exit | `Err`/panic | **error result** | step fail |

The JJK row is the witness for *why* role-must-separate-from-sink: its product fans across two sinks (inline result + gazette side-channel) and its trace goes somewhere the agent never sees. A CLI tool hides all four roles in one stream and lets you pretend the distinction isn't there. MCP forces it.

## The one risk I'd still name

Premature ontology. A fixed role-set risks being too sparse (a project needs a role you didn't name) or too rich (forcing roles nobody emits). The discipline that handles it is ACG's own load-bearing test applied to the *roster*: **a role earns a name only by recurring across projects/languages.** Product/info/trace/error clear that bar in my experience too. **Warn** is the marginal one — I'd hold it as a candidate and make it prove it recurs as distinct from error-and-info before it gets a slot, rather than mint it because syslog has it.

---

I won't touch ACG — design conversation, CMK covenant. But this is clearly an expansion of ACG's execution-time home from one "announcement" into a named role-set with bound plumbing, and it's worth capturing. Want me to draft it as an ACG-amendment pace (its own docket, CMK kit), or keep circling the role roster here first? I'd lean on circling the roster a bit more — "warn" and whether **product** is really design-homed-or-execution-homed both feel unsettled.

message.content: I'd rather formally bring 'nudging' ACG into this pace: delicately done, but present.  That is a document that functions best with small changes.  Else we badly overdesign.  Okay, recommend next steps

content.text:
Recommended — and the recommendation is shaped by the constraint you just set: the smallest true change, deferring the roster the way ACG already defers its ACGm_102 symbol-link dependency ("raised and deferred, not dropped"). That precedent is the model — the document already knows how to name a future expansion in one breath and not build it.

## The nudge, scoped to one paragraph

Insert after the blessed-forms discussion (the failure-path option-disclosure paragraph, ~line 87). **Table untouched** — refine the execution-time home in prose, don't restructure the three-homes frame:

> **The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace** (diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**

Why this is the delicate-but-present version: it opens the home, plants product/trace/error, states *one* load-bearing invariant — and that invariant just extends the temporal-misallocation thread the doc *already* runs for edit-time comments, rather than adding a new axis. The roster, `warn`, and product-vs-design-home all stay deferred in the "future work" clause. No table surgery.

## What it deliberately does *not* trigger

- **No `{jjds_stdout}` quoin rename in the JJK specs.** That's a cross-spec sweep of hot vocabulary — exactly the move ACG rule 1 forbids mid-heat. The four spec repairs just *describe the real channel honestly* in prose (MCP result, + gazette where bulk), citing the principle, without minting.
- **No roster table in ACG.** That was the overdesign you're guarding against.

## Recommended sequence

message.content: Does ACG make direct reference to AXLA and have constraints on how it is used in compliant documents yet?  Seems to me it should but we only drafted it yesterday

content.text:
Direct answer, from the copy I just read: **partially, and lopsidedly.**

## What's actually there

ACG references AXLA in several places — but almost all of them point *inward*, treating AXLA as a dependency of ACG's own moves, not *outward* as constraints on compliant documents:

- **ACGm_102** detects against `//axvo_procedure` / `//axvo_method` voicings, and its "Formal dependency" note waits on an *AXLA arity change* (the `mcm_inlay` symbol lookahead). That's ACG consuming AXLA, and asking AXLA to change — not constraining how a document uses it.
- The **"AXLA/MCM interface"** section + the **lookahead scanning rule** (skip prose, count attribute-refs and sprues). This is the *one* genuine authoring/reading constraint — and it's narrow, scoped to counting lookahead for ACGm_103.
- The **Related Guides** entry just names AXLA as "the motif vocabulary."

So: direct references **yes**; a general normative "here is how a compliant document must use AXLA" contract **no**. You're right — and it's exactly the drafted-yesterday kind of gap.

## The elegant small form (if it's ever drafted)

The minimal true constraint is just **ACG's own spine recursed onto the motif layer**: *reference the home, don't recreate* — applied to motifs themselves. A compliant document voices via AXLA motifs and doesn't mint a local motif AXLA already homes. That's not a new idea bolted on; it's the spine pointed one level up. And it's the *same future* as the deferred output-role roster — when output roles formalize, they'd be AXLA motifs that documents reference, not per-document output vocabulary. Same neighborhood, viewed from the motif-home angle.

## The catch worth naming before anyone drafts it

The home for that constraint may not be ACG. "How a compliant document must use AXLA" reads like **MCM's** job (MCM is the spec governing compliant documents); AXLA is the vocabulary; ACG is allocation. ACG referencing AXLA *for its moves* is clearly ACG's. But the normative document-usage contract might belong in MCM, with ACG only pointing at it. That allocation question should be settled before the constraint is written anywhere — fittingly, since ACG is the allocation guide and would want to follow its own rule.

## Recommendation

**Don't fold this into the current pace.** Two conceptual nudges in one hygiene pace is precisely the "small changes become a redesign" creep you flagged — and this second one isn't even clearly ACG-homed yet. Keep ₢BDAAN's ACG touch to the *one* execution-time paragraph we agreed.

Capture the AXLA-usage-constraint as a separate **itch** — flagged with the open home-question (ACG vs MCM vs AXLA) so the future pace starts from the right allocation decision, not a presumption it lands in ACG.

Want me to log that itch now, then we proceed with the agreed reslate-and-execute on ₢BDAAN? Or keep circling ACG's shape — I sense you're still mapping its edges, and there's no rush to close.

content.text:
Yes — and I think I can name *why* you can't shake it, because the relationship is real and it's at the template level, not just thematic.

## The actual shape of it

These four specs **are operation quoins** — each is the design-time home of a procedure. And `JJSCRL-rail.adoc`, the docket's chosen fidelity model, isn't just "a fuller spec to copy" — it's the **closest existing instance of the routine-specification template ACG aspires to formalize**: operation description, typed-parameter arguments, output contract, a body that *calls* sub-operations, validation errors. So the docket's instruction "bring these to rail-fidelity" is, unknowingly, saying **"conform these procedures to ACG's procedure guidance."** The pace is ACG's procedure mission instantiated *before* ACG formally owns it. Same activity, two altitudes — ACG at design altitude, the pace at execution altitude.

That reframing has a small honest consequence: rail is the best *witness*, not the *authority*. If rail itself carries a non-ACG-conformant habit, I copy the conformant structure, not the habit.

## What it hands me as execution criteria (no scope growth)

Three concrete definitions of "fidelity" beyond matching rail's section skeleton:

1. **Typed-parameter homing (ACGm_103)** — arguments reference their type quoin, consistently. This one's *live and actionable*: the thin specs mix forms — `JJSCGS` writes `` `CORONET` — the {jjdt_coronet} `` (backtick literal *plus* quoin-ref) while rail uses the pure quoin-ref as the argument name. The repair settles that to one convention. Reference the home; don't half-recreate it.
2. **Sub-operation quoin-refs in the body (the spine)** — the behavior section *calls* `{jjdo_log}`, `{jjdo_show}`, `{jjdr_load}` rather than restating their logic. Saddle already does this well; the thin specs are where to check it holds.
3. **Name-identity (ACGm_102)** — and here's where I'll give you the hard read instead of the satisfying one.

## The name-identity finding — it mostly *holds*

I checked, and I won't manufacture a violation. ACGm_102's test is operation-quoin-display-text vs **implementing symbol** — and that's largely intact: `get-spec` ↔ `jjrgs_get_spec.rs`, `parade` ↔ `jjrpd_parade.rs`, `saddle` ↔ `jjrsd_saddle.rs`. Conformant.

The naming spread that *looks* like a problem — `brief`/`coronets`/`show` (MCP verbs) vs `get-spec`/`get-coronets`/`parade` (operation names) — is a **different axis**: public-API-verb vs internal-operation-name. ACGm_102 doesn't govern it (the symbol matches the operation, which is what it checks). So it's a real phenomenon worth a one-line note in the repair, but **not** an ACG violation to fix, and definitely not a renaming sweep (that'd be the hot-vocabulary move rule 1 forbids). Report it; leave it.

## How it sits with the plan

It doesn't expand the pace — it *sharpens execution*, which is the carry-it-as-how-I-work posture you favored. And it's coherent with the nudge: the execution-time-home paragraph handles the **output-role** facet of a procedure; this handles the **whole-procedure** facet. Two faces of the one routine-spec idea.

Net change to ₢BDAAN: none to scope. The working definition of "rail-fidelity" becomes "ACG procedure-conformance, with rail as witness" — typed-param homing settled, body quoin-refs verified, name-identity noted-not-touched.

Ready to proceed: reslate (the one ACG paragraph) + execute the four repairs under that definition. Your nod?
